calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters are heard concurrently:
CGC-09-275391 Margarita Flores, et al. VS. Asbestos Defendants.
CGC-11-275860 Dominic Rielli VS. Certaineed Corporation.

APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Grant of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
K. Albert Loquaci (415-890-5252) and Ethan H. Seibert (415-890-5184) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP appearing for Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company.

RULING:
Hearing held, ARGUED. Moving party states they have not denied coverage, is continuing investigation of coverage, and requests court to take notice of 11 exhibits. The Court GRANTS the request for Judicial Notice. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.)

Order signed in chambers.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant, CSR 906 (Tel: 510-757-8550; email: npresantcsr@gmail.com). (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Oren P. Noah, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, DOMICIC RIELLI.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiff, DOMINIC RIELLI, shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $422,700.00 in Economic Damages, $750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $1,172,700.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Off Calendar Apr-24-2012 Continued To Aug-16-12 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jun-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties
Michael E. Sandgren of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / opposing party
Kenneth D. Hollenbeck of LAW OFFICES OF LUCINDA L. STORM ( 415-777-6990), appearing for defendant / opposing party
Allison E. Mullings of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & MCCALL (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / opposing party

RULING:
On the Court's own discretion, the Court will hear arguments on this motion even though two additional days for E-service was not provided by the moving party. Argued and the Court grants Plaintiff's Motion for Preference pursuant to CCP 36 (a). Trial date is 10/1/12 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 503. Parties stipulated to the last day for hearing summary motions is 9/20/12 and that summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Oppositions are due five court days before the hearing and replies due two court days before the hearing. Plaintiff is to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than 8/31/12. Settlement conference is set in room 603 at 1:30pm on 9/20/12. Fact discovery cut-off date is 9/14/12. Expert discovery cut-off date is 9/28/12. No later than 6/29/12, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than 6/29/12, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference is set on 9/12/12 at 1:30pm. in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. Mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 is set on 10/1/12 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. All clients are to be present unless permission is granted otherwise. Written order pending. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436, AIKEN WELCH COURT REPORTERS (510-451-1580) (503, TLJ)
